commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(model-3d): add splitFaceByPolygon kernel function

Implements the core polygon-split algorithm that all drawing-on-face
tools (Rectangle, Circle, Arc, Polygon) will use. For each polygon edge,
extends it to an infinite line and splits all tracked faces sequentially,
then identifies the inner face by centroid proximity.

Also fixes a critical bug in splitFaceByLine where the t parameter passed
to splitEdge was not adjusted for shared edges whose stored half-edge
direction is opposite to the face's half-edge direction. This caused
splits on box faces (or any face with shared edges) to create vertices
at the wrong positions (1-t instead of t).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

commit.message:
Fix cloud sync: await pushToCloud, add updatedAt timestamps, prevent stale overwrites

Three related fixes for cloud sync reliability:

1. syncAfterWrite now awaits pushToCloud instead of fire-and-forget,
   ensuring Firestore writes complete and errors are logged
2. All write functions (tasks, meetings, instances, people, etc.) now
   set updatedAt timestamps on every change
3. The onSnapshot listener checks updatedAt before overwriting — if
   local data is newer than cloud data, it keeps the local version
   and re-pushes to cloud instead of overwriting

Root cause: pushToCloud was fire-and-forget with a silent catch {},
so failed Firestore writes went unnoticed. On page refresh, the
onSnapshot initial snapshot would pull stale cloud data and
overwrite correct local changes via table.put().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix formatter byte offset calculation and remove formatTaggedTemplates flag (#342)

## Summary
- **Fix byte offset calculation**: Use `SwcSpanConverter` for callback
builder position calculation to correctly handle multi-byte characters
(CJK, emoji)
- **Remove `formatTaggedTemplates` flag**: Tagged template formatting is
now always enabled — the `--format-tagged-templates` CLI flag and
`formatTaggedTemplates` option are removed
- **Fix CLI check mode exit codes**: Return exit code 0 when all files
are formatted, exit code 1 when parse errors exist

## Test plan
- [x] Added unit tests for multi-byte character handling (Japanese
comments, emoji)
- [x] Added idempotency tests (format → format returns `modified:
false`)
- [x] Added CLI integration tests for check mode exit codes with parse
errors
- [x] Added CLI integration test for tagged template formatting without
flag
- [x] Updated existing tagged template tests to remove
`formatTaggedTemplates` parameter
- [x] Verified `graphql` is now a required (non-optional) peer
dependency of formatter

commit.message:
feat: 3-phase ULW execution — Team → QA → PM sign-off

Phase 1: Team execution (spawn agents in parallel)
Phase 2: QA verification (read files, run tests, auto-add test-engineer if missing)
Phase 3: PM sign-off (CTO-level review, reject → retry cycle)

QA team is now always included. PM review asks "would I ship this?"
If any phase fails, loops back with learnings. Max 3 full cycles.

commit.message:
v3 poison_mushroom 01: サンプル生成(工程1 Phase1-5) 全検証PASS

- T導出命題D-8(食用キノコも生える)を因果充足性検証で抽出
- 2ピース構成: P-LOST(独立)→P-REASON(依存・キーピース)
- 9問の質問で遭難→毒キノコの推論→救助の見込みへ到達
- entailmentチェーン: D-3→D-4→D-5, {D-5,D-1}→D-6→D-7

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: strip ReactFlow internals from persisted state to resolve React #185

Root cause: Zustand persist middleware saved full ReactFlow node objects
including internal properties (measured, width, height, selected, dragging,
resizing, internals). On hydration, xyflow's internal store detected stale
measured values and entered an infinite reconciliation loop.

Three fixes:
- partialize: strip nodes to {id,type,position,data}, edges to core props
- onNodesChange/onEdgesChange: whitelist approach — only position/remove/
  add/replace/reset pass through; dimensions and select are ReactFlow-internal
- merge function: cleans legacy persisted data on hydration

Also removes dead CanvasActionsContext.tsx (replaced by direct Zustand selectors).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  - 分類設定を TTL キャッシュで取得（Worker と同パターン）
  - classifyAndSaveIntent() でバッチ書き込み（IntentRecord + processedAt を 1 ラウンドトリップ）
  - 既存メッセージで processedAt: null の場合は再分類を試みる（永久スタック解消）
- salary-drafts: PATCH スキーマから金額フィールドを除去してヘッダ/明細の乖離を防止
  - effectiveDate / reason のみ更新可能
- テスト: chat-sync モックに classifyIntent / db.batch / intentRecords を追加

Co-authored-by: yasushihonda-acg <248675557+yasushihonda-acg@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace details/summary with button/div to fix iOS Safari hamburger menu

iOS Safari fires a native <details> toggle on touchend before the synthetic
click, causing [open] to be in the wrong state by the time our JS runs.
Using a plain <button> eliminates this entirely — buttons have no native
toggle behaviour, so iOS cannot interfere. Submenus still use <details>.

commit.message:
Add fog regions, NL curriculum, expanded schema, and docs

Major features:
- Fog regions: excluded sub-types in projections collapse into 1×1
  "fog" vectors with self-awareness and parent connectivity, maintaining
  representation of unmodeled causal structure
- Natural language curriculum: CurriculumSpec/Stage/Subject system lets
  training DAGs be specified in YAML/Python with NL subject descriptions
  resolved to field paths via keyword matching
- Schema expansion: 7 new layers (biology, infrastructure, cyber, space,
  health, education, legal) — 857 → 951 fields
- 4 new data collectors: World Bank, NOAA Climate, IMF, BIS

Minor improvements:
- Named temporal constants (TICK, HOURLY, DAILY, etc.) replace raw ints
- .env loading in conftest.py so live tests don't skip
- mkdocs documentation site with GitHub Pages workflow
- 34 new tests (12 fog + 22 NL curriculum), 140 total passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: conversational UX improvements — confirmation gate, swap, stall recovery

1. Confirmation step before checkout: after items + delivery + observation,
   show order summary and ask CONFIRMAR/EDITAR/CANCELAR before collecting
   customer data. EDITAR re-opens order for modifications, CANCELAR resets.

2. Swap order modification: "cambia la hamburguesa por una doble" swaps
   items preserving quantity (Pattern 5 in TryParseOrderModification).

3. Conversation recovery: detect ambiguous stall responses (mmm, no se,
   espera, que tienen) and gently redirect with order summary or examples
   instead of wasting an AI parse call.

4. 32 new tests covering confirmation flow, edit/cancel commands, swap
   pattern parsing, and stall detection.
